author: kiui
patch:
commit fe8d12cff8c827df7bb77a3c8e8b37408cb6fe4c
Author: kiui <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 3 11:27:53 2024 +0800

    Update readme.md
---
 readme.md | 9 +++------
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

diff --git a/readme.md b/readme.md
index 1d84c0c..e29cb2b 100644
--- a/readme.md
+++ b/readme.md
@@ -7,11 +7,8 @@ This is the official implementation of *LGM: Large Multi-View Gaussian Model for
 
 https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/assets/25863658/cf64e489-29f3-4935-adba-e393a24c26e8
 
-> [!NOTE]
-> Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi) and [@florinshen](https://github.com/florinshen), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
-> However, the current model is trained with this wrong code and correcting it leads to some quality degradation. We will work on retraining a correct model in the future.
-> 
-> **If you are going to retrain a model, please correct it to `self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)`** !
+### News
+[2024.4.3] Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi) and [@florinshen](https://github.com/florinshen), we have fixed a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/commit/9a0797cdbacf8e6216d0108cb00cbe43b9cb3d81). We have finetuned the model with correct normalization for 30 more epochs and uploaded new checkpoints.
 
 ### Replicate Demo:
 * gaussians: [demo](https://replicate.com/camenduru/lgm) | [code](https://github.com/camenduru/LGM-replicate)
@@ -45,7 +42,7 @@ Our pretrained weight can be downloaded from [huggingface](https://huggingface.c
 For example, to download the fp16 model for inference:
 ```bash
 mkdir pretrained && cd pretrained
-wget https://huggingface.co/ashawkey/LGM/resolve/main/model_fp16.safetensors
+wget https://huggingface.co/ashawkey/LGM/resolve/main/model_fp16_fixrot.safetensors
 cd ..
 ```

author: ashawkey
patch:
commit 9a0797cdbacf8e6216d0108cb00cbe43b9cb3d81
Author: ashawkey <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 3 11:23:18 2024 +0800

    fix rotation normalization
---
 core/models.py | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/core/models.py b/core/models.py
index c61b7b6..5b8bf32 100644
--- a/core/models.py
+++ b/core/models.py
@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ class LGM(nn.Module):
         self.pos_act = lambda x: x.clamp(-1, 1)
         self.scale_act = lambda x: 0.1 * F.softplus(x)
         self.opacity_act = lambda x: torch.sigmoid(x)
-        self.rot_act = F.normalize
+        self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)
         self.rgb_act = lambda x: 0.5 * torch.tanh(x) + 0.5 # NOTE: may use sigmoid if train again
 
         # LPIPS loss

author: kiui
patch:
commit 310e0dced01a1b6cee83cc9ba92daca0fe67749c
Author: kiui <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 29 13:51:36 2024 +0800

    Update readme.md
---
 readme.md | 10 +++++-----
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/readme.md b/readme.md
index 653a543..1d84c0c 100644
--- a/readme.md
+++ b/readme.md
@@ -7,11 +7,11 @@ This is the official implementation of *LGM: Large Multi-View Gaussian Model for
 
 https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/assets/25863658/cf64e489-29f3-4935-adba-e393a24c26e8
 
-### Notice
-Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi) and [@florinshen](https://github.com/florinshen), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
-However, the current model is trained with this wrong code and correcting it leads to some quality degradation. We will work on retraining a correct model in the future.
-
-**If you are going to retrain a model, please correct it to `self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)`** !
+> [!NOTE]
+> Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi) and [@florinshen](https://github.com/florinshen), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
+> However, the current model is trained with this wrong code and correcting it leads to some quality degradation. We will work on retraining a correct model in the future.
+> 
+> **If you are going to retrain a model, please correct it to `self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)`** !
 
 ### Replicate Demo:
 * gaussians: [demo](https://replicate.com/camenduru/lgm) | [code](https://github.com/camenduru/LGM-replicate)

author: kiui
patch:
commit 0a7a857f4e67eb1ec108329ae672479f5884fda6
Author: kiui <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 29 11:06:28 2024 +0800

    Update readme.md
---
 readme.md | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/readme.md b/readme.md
index 6a59348..653a543 100644
--- a/readme.md
+++ b/readme.md
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@ This is the official implementation of *LGM: Large Multi-View Gaussian Model for
 https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/assets/25863658/cf64e489-29f3-4935-adba-e393a24c26e8
 
 ### Notice
-Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
+Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi) and [@florinshen](https://github.com/florinshen), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
 However, the current model is trained with this wrong code and correcting it leads to some quality degradation. We will work on retraining a correct model in the future.
 
 **If you are going to retrain a model, please correct it to `self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)`** !

author: kiui
message: notice a bug in rotation normalization.
patch:
commit 0162cb84dc693784a9a701b9a7fccbab65bf5a67
Author: kiui <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Mar 29 10:36:07 2024 +0800

    notice a bug in rotation normalization.
---
 readme.md | 8 +++++++-
 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/readme.md b/readme.md
index e222156..6a59348 100644
--- a/readme.md
+++ b/readme.md
@@ -7,11 +7,17 @@ This is the official implementation of *LGM: Large Multi-View Gaussian Model for
 
 https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/assets/25863658/cf64e489-29f3-4935-adba-e393a24c26e8
 
+### Notice
+Thanks to [@yxymessi](https://github.com/yxymessi), we have found that there is a **severe bug in rotation normalization** [here](https://github.com/3DTopia/LGM/blob/main/core/models.py#L43).
+However, the current model is trained with this wrong code and correcting it leads to some quality degradation. We will work on retraining a correct model in the future.
+
+**If you are going to retrain a model, please correct it to `self.rot_act = lambda x: F.normalize(x, dim=-1)`** !
+
 ### Replicate Demo:
 * gaussians: [demo](https://replicate.com/camenduru/lgm) | [code](https://github.com/camenduru/LGM-replicate)
 * mesh: [demo](https://replicate.com/camenduru/lgm-ply-to-glb) | [code](https://github.com/camenduru/LGM-ply-to-glb-replicate)
 
-Thanks [@camenduru](https://github.com/camenduru)!
+Thanks to [@camenduru](https://github.com/camenduru)!
 
 ### Install

author: kiui
patch:
commit be8daf1264dd878e47c43162427fa4237b4bad41
Author: kiui <ashawkey1999@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 29 16:02:44 2024 +0800

    fix training background to white
---
 core/models.py | 11 ++++-------
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

diff --git a/core/models.py b/core/models.py
index 382deec..c61b7b6 100644
--- a/core/models.py
+++ b/core/models.py
@@ -131,12 +131,9 @@ class LGM(nn.Module):
 
         results['gaussians'] = gaussians
 
-        # random bg for training
-        if self.training:
-            bg_color = torch.rand(3, dtype=torch.float32, device=gaussians.device)
-        else:
-            bg_color = torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32, device=gaussians.device)
-
+        # always use white bg
+        bg_color = torch.ones(3, dtype=torch.float32, device=gaussians.device)
+        
         # use the other views for rendering and supervision
         results = self.gs.render(gaussians, data['cam_view'], data['cam_view_proj'], data['cam_pos'], bg_color=bg_color)
         pred_images = results['image'] # [B, V, C, output_size, output_size]
@@ -171,4 +168,4 @@ class LGM(nn.Module):
             psnr = -10 * torch.log10(torch.mean((pred_images.detach() - gt_images) ** 2))
             results['psnr'] = psnr
 
-        return results
\ No newline at end of file
+        return results

--- a/readme.md
+++ b/readme.md
@@ -68,6 +68,8 @@ For more options, please check [options](./core/options.py).
 Since the dataset used in our training is based on AWS, it cannot be directly used for training in a new environment.
 We provide the necessary training code framework, please check and modify the [dataset](./core/provider_objaverse.py) implementation!
 
+We also provide the **~80K subset of [Objaverse](https://objaverse.allenai.org/objaverse-1.0)** used to train LGM in [objaverse_filter](https://github.com/ashawkey/objaverse_filter).

patch:
commit 58d93924e2d9b1b4906a5444010be9dd6f6d0479
Author: Brent Yi <yibrenth@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 8 02:13:28 2024 -0800

    Type / import nits
---
 core/options.py | 14 +++++++-------
 core/unet.py    | 14 +++++++-------
 2 files changed, 14 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)

diff --git a/core/options.py b/core/options.py
index c2b6da1..4cc3194 100644
--- a/core/options.py
+++ b/core/options.py
@@ -9,16 +9,16 @@ class Options:
     # Unet image input size
     input_size: int = 256
     # Unet definition
-    down_channels: Tuple[int] = (64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1024)
-    down_attention: Tuple[bool] = (False, False, False, True, True, True)
+    down_channels: Tuple[int, ...] = (64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1024)
+    down_attention: Tuple[bool, ...] = (False, False, False, True, True, True)
     mid_attention: bool = True
-    up_channels: Tuple[int] = (1024, 1024, 512, 256)
-    up_attention: Tuple[bool] = (True, True, True, False)
+    up_channels: Tuple[int, ...] = (1024, 1024, 512, 256)
+    up_attention: Tuple[bool, ...] = (True, True, True, False)
     # Unet output size, dependent on the input_size and U-Net structure!
     splat_size: int = 64
     # gaussian render size
     output_size: int = 256
-    
+
     ### dataset
     # data mode (only support s3 now)
     data_mode: Literal['s3'] = 's3'
@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ class Options:
     ### training
     # workspace
     workspace: str = './workspace'
-    # resume 
+    # resume
     resume: Optional[str] = None
     # batch size (per-GPU)
     batch_size: int = 8
@@ -117,4 +117,4 @@ config_defaults['tiny'] = Options(
     mixed_precision='bf16',
 )
 
-AllConfigs = tyro.extras.subcommand_type_from_defaults(config_defaults, config_doc)
\ No newline at end of file
+AllConfigs = tyro.extras.subcommand_type_from_defaults(config_defaults, config_doc)
diff --git a/core/unet.py b/core/unet.py
index 2945bf6..4134e80 100644
--- a/core/unet.py
+++ b/core/unet.py
@@ -3,10 +3,10 @@ import torch.nn as nn
 import torch.nn.functional as F
 
 import numpy as np
-from typing import Tuple, Optional, Literal
+from typing import Tuple, Literal
 from functools import partial
 
-from core.attention import MemEffAttention, MemEffCrossAttention
+from core.attention import MemEffAttention
 
 class MVAttention(nn.Module):
     def __init__(
@@ -236,11 +236,11 @@ class UNet(nn.Module):
         self,
         in_channels: int = 3,
         out_channels: int = 3,
-        down_channels: Tuple[int] = (64, 128, 256, 512, 1024),
-        down_attention: Tuple[bool] = (False, False, False, True, True),
+        down_channels: Tuple[int, ...] = (64, 128, 256, 512, 1024),
+        down_attention: Tuple[bool, ...] = (False, False, False, True, True),
         mid_attention: bool = True,
-        up_channels: Tuple[int] = (1024, 512, 256),
-        up_attention: Tuple[bool] = (True, True, False),
+        up_channels: Tuple[int, ...] = (1024, 512, 256),
+        up_attention: Tuple[bool, ...] = (True, True, False),
         layers_per_block: int = 2,
         skip_scale: float = np.sqrt(0.5),
     ):
@@ -316,4 +316,4 @@ class UNet(nn.Module):
         x = F.silu(x)
         x = self.conv_out(x) # [B, Cout, H', W']
 
-        return x
\ No newline at end of file
+        return x
